SENDCLOUD × GLS CONFIG FOR THE HAUTE FIDÉLITÉ SHOPIFY STORE
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/journal/config-sendcloud-gls-hf.html
Published: 2026-04-03
================================================================

Pooling HF e-commerce logistics with the existing Cobra infrastructure (Sendcloud + daily GLS pickup) while keeping HF-branded customer tracking. Real-time guided config.

← Journal
Haute Fidélité · Logistics
Sendcloud × GLS config for the Haute Fidélité Shopify store
Claude · HL · April 3, 2026 · Haute Fidélité Haute Fidélitélogistiqueen-cours
Context
I wanted to pool Haute Fidélité's e-commerce logistics with the infrastructure already in place at Cobra (shared Sendcloud account + daily GLS pickup), while keeping distinct, HF-branded customer tracking.
What was built
- Confirmed that the multi-store Sendcloud account works (HF Shopify already connected)
- Carrier selected: GLS (existing daily pickup), parcels 300–600g
- Decision: no additional insurance, basic GLS coverage is sufficient
- Config started: Connected Stores page, next step = tracking branding
What was hard / unexpected
- GLS doesn't deliver to mailboxes — option quickly ruled out
- Tracking branding isn't under Settings but in a dedicated Tracking menu
Stack
Sendcloud, GLS, Shopify.
What this illustrates
Real-time operational config session with screenshots — Claude reads the interface, rules out dead ends (mailbox/GLS) and sequences steps without overwhelming.
Still to do
- Locate and configure HF tracking branding in Sendcloud
- Create the automatic GLS shipping rule on the HF store
- Verify that the Cobra GLS contract is accessible from the HF store

================================================================
DOCS &AMP; BILLING FOR THE SALON HAUTE FIDÉLITÉ PARIS 2026
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/journal/docs-facturation-salon-hf-2026.html
Published: 2026-03-15
================================================================

Streamlining the admin side of the HF 2026 show: dropping the formal contract in favor of a Qonto invoice + bilingual participation terms, for 20–30 exhibitors.

← Journal
Haute Fidélité · Document production
Docs & billing for the Salon Haute Fidélité Paris 2026
Claude · HL · March 2026 · Haute Fidélité Haute Fidélitédocadminen-prod
Context
I was preparing the HF 2026 show and wanted to simplify the admin compared to 2025 — drop the formal contract, centralize everything in a Qonto invoice + a generic terms document. Save time across 20–30 exhibitors while staying legally covered.
What was built
- Bilingual FR/EN "Participation Terms" PDF, 2 pages — 10 articles (exhibitor space, payment, cancellation, insurance, image rights, force majeure, GDPR, jurisdiction)
- "What's included" box in 2 columns (Technical + Communications)
- 2026 visual identity respected: navy #1C2F6B, beige #C2A882, FR/EN badges
- Qonto billing workflow documented (50% deposit + balance, mandatory invoice fields)
- Two email templates: standard send and a 100%-upfront version for slow payers
What was tricky / unexpected
- ReportLab doesn't support complex layouts without iterating — several passes to get text and columns to sit right
- The old contract contained a subletting clause without prior consent: spotted and removed without being explicitly asked to do so
Stack
Python, ReportLab, Qonto (native invoicing).
What this shows
A "complete document production" session — from scratch to send-ready deliverables, with legal decisions, visual identity, and operational workflow all handled in the same conversation.
See also
- Audit & launch of the Haute Fidélité Shopify store
- Sendcloud × GLS config for Haute Fidélité Shopify
- Daily HiFi monitoring Telegram bot
← All entries Haute Fidélité project →


================================================================
DAILY HIFI NEWS TELEGRAM BOT
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/journal/bot-telegram-veille-hifi.html
Published: 2026-03-07
================================================================

A Telegram push every morning with worldwide hi-fi news: ~35 multilingual RSS sources, written by the Claude API, duplicate-memory, GitHub Actions cron — from scratch.

← Journal
Haute Fidélité · Automation
Daily HiFi news Telegram bot
Claude · HL · March 7, 2026 · Haute Fidélité Haute Fidélitéagentdataen-cours
Context
I wanted to receive a summary of major worldwide hi-fi news every morning, without email feeds or paid subscriptions. The goal: an automatic push, free, multilingual, with duplicate memory, covering FR/EN/DE/JP/CN/KR.
What was built
- Python script veille_hifi.py scraping ~35 global RSS sources (Stereophile, Darko Audio, What Hi-Fi, Analog Planet, multilingual Google News…)
- Automatic selection and writing by Claude Sonnet via the Anthropic API + daily music anecdote
- Telegram bot @hautefidelite_veille_bot receiving the brief every morning at 8am Paris time
- Executed via GitHub Actions (cron 0 7 * * *), persistent duplicate memory in seen_news.json, fallback message
What was hard / unexpected
- Several YAML errors in daily.yml related to ${{ }} syntax in bash commands — resolved by isolating variables in an env: block
- The seen_news.json file created in the wrong place (inside .github/workflows/) was breaking the git step
Stack
Python 3.11, feedparser, anthropic SDK, requests, GitHub Actions, Telegram Bot API.
What this illustrates
Full build of an automated monitoring tool from scratch in a single session, with no prior GitHub Actions experience — Claude drives each debugging step in real time from log screenshots.
Still to do
- Deploy v4: date in French, 👍/👎 buttons per topic with preference memory, editorial weighting (less vinyl, more gear/brands)
See also
- Audit & launch of the Haute Fidélité Shopify store
- Sendcloud × GLS config for Haute Fidélité Shopify
- Docs & invoicing — Salon Haute Fidélité Paris 2026
← All entries Haute Fidélité project →


================================================================
COLLEAGUES START USING CLAUDE
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/journal/collegues-sur-claude.html
Published: 2025-09-01
================================================================

Claude isn't just a solo tool anymore: colleagues at Cobra are starting to use it. Sharing context without sharing credentials, a good CLAUDE.md, avoiding reinventing the wheel.

← Journal
Cobra · Organizational / culture
Colleagues start using Claude
Claude · Cobra · 2025–2026 · Cobra metaen-cours
What's happening
Claude isn't just a solo tool anymore. Colleagues at Cobra are starting to use it — first for one-off questions, then for more structured tasks.
The challenges
- How do you share context without sharing credentials?
- How do you make sure everyone's using the right CLAUDE.md?
- How do you stop people reinventing the wheel on topics that are already documented?
What's in place (or in progress)
- CLAUDE.md per project with rules and context
- Separation of work account vs personal account
- Knowledge Hub as a shared reference: colleagues read the documented patterns here
<em>Cette entrée est volontairement incomplète — à enrichir quand le déploiement collègues sera plus avancé.</em>
← All entries Cobra project →


================================================================
CLAUDE JOINS THE COBRA WORKFLOW
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/journal/claude-arrivee-workflow-cobra.html
Published: 2025-01-15
================================================================

Before Claude, Odoo dev was manual: OCA forums, official docs, trial and error. The pivot — treating Claude as a senior colleague who amplifies, not replaces.

← Journal
Cobra · Founding story
Claude joins the Cobra workflow
Claude · Cobra · early 2025 · Cobra odoo-devmetaen-prod
What happened
Before Claude, Odoo development was manual: searching OCA forums, reading official docs, trial and error. Slow. Every customization took days.
First use: questions about Odoo 17 module structure. Then 18. Then code generation requests. Then full sessions where Claude writes the module, manages XML migrations, and diagnoses console errors.
The pivot: realizing that Claude doesn't replace the developer — it amplifies the ability to explore, test, and document. A solo dev can operate like a small team.
What changed
- Odoo development speed: cut by a factor of 3 to 4
- Ability to document in real time (CLAUDE.md per project)
- Exploration of topics outside my comfort zone (JS OWL, XML, advanced Python)
"I started treating Claude as a senior colleague available 24/7 — not an enhanced search engine, but someone you give context to and who proposes a direction."
← All entries Cobra project →


================================================================
ABOUT
URL: https://hl-consulting.tech/knowledge-hub/en/a-propos/
================================================================

Hugo Lahutte: 17 years at Son-Vidéo.com (from €10M to €62M), now CEO of Cobra, publisher of Haute Fidélité magazine and founder of HL Consulting. Background, projects, and a way of working with Claude.

About
HugoLahutte
In love with audio, image and anything that stirs emotion. 17 years growing Son-Vidéo.com, today CEO of Cobra, publisher of Haute Fidélité and founder of HL Consulting — and I now build nearly all of my tools and content with Claude.
At my desk, at Cobra.
My background
It all starts with a passion: music and cinema. As a student, I sold electronics at Darty, then dipped into events and Japanese culture by helping organize Japan Expo for five years — stage manager of the main hall, logistics, sound. You learn to adapt to anything there.
Then came the defining adventure: 16 years at Son-Vidéo.com, France's number-one online hi-fi and home-cinema retailer. I touched everything — purchasing, sales, commercial strategy, marketing, advertising (on and offline), brand creation and import. Head of purchasing, then of marketing communications, then of commercial strategy, then of partnerships. That's where I learned how to take a company from €10M to €62M, and where I built a network across the whole industry.
My story, told on LinkedIn.
The projects so far
Since 2024, I've put all of that experience to work on my own projects — coherent, on a single field: exceptional audio and image.
Since Oct. 2024 · CEO Cobra An iconic HiFi and Home Cinema brand. I'm leading its transformation: repositioning, retail & e-commerce experience (cobra.fr), digitalization. It's also my Claude Code playground. See the project → Since Jan. 2025 · Publisher Haute Fidélité Taking over and relaunching the reference magazine of 30+ years — the last specialist title on newsstands. New 2025 format: high standards, emotion, experience. Premium print + digital presence. See the project → Since Jul. 2024 · Founder HL Consulting My personal structure: the thread running through my projects, my AI explorations and everything I share here. The point of contact if you want to talk. Connect →
What keeps me going
An unapologetic geek, in love with music and cinema, drawn to design and beautiful objects — my desk looks like my interests (Star Wars, Dune, vinyl, gorgeous speakers…). That world feeds directly into HiFi Lovers.
What drives me today is passing it on: showing how AI concretely changes a team's day-to-day, and helping as many people as possible get their hands on it. That's the whole point of this Knowledge Hub and of my method.
The world: music, cinema, design, pop culture.
Let's talk
I document all of this in public and I love talking about it. A question about my method, a project that intrigues you, or just the urge to compare approaches? Drop me a line: hugo@hl-consulting.tech. We can also follow each other on LinkedIn and X.
Read my method → See the projects
